policy: This is a planned beat ledger, not a runnable story pack. Local chapter descriptions establish the six chapter outcomes. Panel composition, exact choreography, and unsupported topology remain open research. The runtime must preserve the chapter-102 unidentified-whale state before revealing the name Laboon in chapter 103.

source_policy.warning: The local vessel and voyage-leg files explicitly mark their hand-authored chapter stamps verified:false. They can supply identity and ordering context, not a newly asserted exact gate.

scenes.outcome_claim: The crew leaves Loguetown and heads for the Grand Line after Dragon stops Smoker amid the storm.
scenes.coverage_action: Reuse the existing East Blue chapter-100 finale/approach coverage as the opening handoff; do not build a duplicate combat scene in this W1 lane.
scenes.camera_plan: End the East Blue scene over open sea and point toward the Grand Line route without rendering the mountain.

scenes.outcome_claim: After accidentally reaching the Calm Belt, the crew returns to the intended route and heads up Reverse Mountain into the Grand Line.
scenes.coverage_action: Build as the first moving 3D voyage proof, with the signed 2.5D crew visible only in close cockpit/deck inserts.
scenes.camera_plan: Follow low behind the Merry, bank with the ascent spline, then widen at the crest. Cut before any chapter-102 whale or Twin Cape reveal.

scenes.outcome_claim: The crew arrives in the Grand Line and the Going Merry is swallowed by a giant whale.
scenes.coverage_action: Build the world-scale Laboon silhouette and swallow transition, but suppress the character name until chapter 103.
scenes.camera_plan: Exit the descent at water level, let the whale silhouette take over the frame, then hide the transition inside mouth occlusion and moisture haze.
do_not_infer: name Laboon in UI or narration

scenes.outcome_claim: Inside the whale, the crew meets Crocus, learns the whale is named Laboon, and also encounters a strange man and woman.
scenes.coverage_action: Use a contained 3D inset for scale and atmosphere with signed 2.5D character performances. Preserve the two agents as unnamed chapter-safe silhouettes.
scenes.camera_plan: Open wide enough to establish the impossible interior setting, then settle into a compact Crocus explanation tableau before returning outside.
do_not_infer: agent identities beyond the local summary

scenes.outcome_claim: After the crew emerges, Luffy promises Laboon that he will return after conquering the Grand Line.
scenes.coverage_action: Treat this as the emotional payoff: large 3D world/creature staging plus a signed Luffy promise performance, not as an invented extended fight.
scenes.camera_plan: Compose the Merry and Luffy as small foreground scale against Laboon, then finish on Laboon's response and the open Grand Line horizon.
audio_categories: original promise motif without copyrighted melody

scenes.outcome_claim: Crocus gives the crew a Log Pose, and the Straw Hats agree to take Mr. 9 and Miss Wednesday to Whisky Peak.
scenes.coverage_action: Use the Log Pose as the close story insert, then animate the Merry out of the local theatre and hand route ownership back to the globe.
scenes.camera_plan: Start close on the Log Pose exchange, pull to reveal the two passengers on the Merry, then follow the departure until the globe route takes over.
do_not_infer: exact local bearing to Whisky Peak
later Vivi identity context beyond the chapter-105 names

batch_acceptance: Every chapter from 100 through 105 appears exactly once.
Every chapter row remains pinned to the current local title and description hash.
Chapter 100 has no Reverse Mountain environment leak.
Chapter 102 labels the creature only as an unidentified giant whale.
Chapter 103 is the first scene allowed to display Laboon and Crocus by name.
Chapter 104 stays a promise scene and does not invent an extended battle.
Chapter 105 exits through the existing world-route system rather than creating a second map authority.
All audio categories are placeholders for original or licensed material; no anime soundtrack or dialogue is bundled.
All panel-dependent geometry and choreography remains explicitly unresolved until a separate reference pass.
